solution.text:
xmain = 2   26 

ymain = 5   12 

1) ymain = jumble(5,2) 


x=12, y=2 


x=2*x+y 


x=2*5+2 


x=12 


after 1st jumble function call 


xmain=2, ymain=12 

2) xmain=jumble(12,2) 


x=26,y=2 


x=2*x+y 


x=2*12+2 


x=26 


after 2nd jumble function call 


xmain=26, and ymain=12 


lastly value of x is printed which is 26.

questions.subject: Programming and Data Structures
questions.subtopic: The value printed by the program is ___________.
tags: The value printed by the program is ___________.

solution.text:
r(): it will return the current value of num and returns 
num=num-1(post decrement).  



Post-decrement: A post-decrement operator is used 
to decrement the value of a variable after executing 
the expression in which the operator is used. 

(1) r() will return 7 and then num is decreased by 1 
that is num =7, value 7 is printed and then 
decremented. 

(2) r() will return 6(which is non zero) that's why 
condition will become true and code inside the 
loop will be executed, but before reaching printf 
statement num is decresed by 1 ie…. num 
becomes 5. 

(3) printf("%d"r()); 


r() inside printf will return 5 and same is printed 
and after printing num becomes 4. 

(4) r() is called and 4 is returned and then decreses 
the num value by 1, hence num becomes 3. 


(5) r() will return 3 which is non zero(true) so printf 
will be executed, before printf num value 
becomes 2 because of post decrement. 

(6) printf("%d",r()), r() returns 2 and same value is 
printed. And then num becomes 1. 

(7) r(), will return 1 and decrements the value to 
zero. 

(8) r(), Here zero is returned and condition of for 
loop becomes false hence loop gets terminated. 

Therefore, output printed is 52 which is option b.

*  If y =1, then the function will terminate without 
printing anything. 

Count (x, 1) will not print anything 


else statement will be executed when if condition 
fails.

y = y - 1 ⇒ y = 1023

10 times

options.text: The function returns 0 for all values of j.

The function prints the string something for all 
values of j.

The function will exhaust the runtime stack or 
run into an infinite loop when j = 50. 

Recursion

solution.text:
After execution of funcp 2 times, printf function 
prints the output (7) and gets deleted from activation 
record. 

Similarly, control is returned to the main function, 
main function also gets deleted from activation 
record and control is returned to operating system.  

Hence 7 is printed.

Comparing the above tree with the given trees in 
options. Above tree matches with the tree given in 
option A, hence a is correct answer.

questions.subject: Programming and Data Structures
questions.subtopic: The value returned by SomeFunction(15, 255) is____.
tags: The value returned by SomeFunction(15, 255) is____.

solution.text:
Lets first understand the fragments of code. 

1) if((x \le 0)&&(y \le 0)) 


The above case states that when both x and y are 
less than 0 (or) equal to zero then return 3rd 
argument(q). 

2) if(x \le 0)  


The above case states that x < 0 and y > 0 then 
decrement 2nd argument using 3rd argument. 

3) if(y < 0) 

The above case states that x >0, y < 0 then decrement 
first argument using 3rd argument.   

4) return foo (x, y - q, q) + foo (x - q, y, a) 

The above case states that, when both x, y > 0 then 
from first function call, decrease 2nd argument using 
3rd argument and from 2nd function call, decrease first 
argument using 3rd argument.

tags: The output printed when fun1(5) is called is

options.text:
Infinite 
loop 
and 
abnormal 
termination 
respectively

Abnormal 
termination 
and 
infinite 
loop 
respectively

solution.text:
Understanding foo function 

x=x+foo(val--) ; 

val--performs post decrement.foo(val) will be called 
and then val=val-1 is performed later(after function 
call) 


foo(3) will call foo(3) which will again call foo(3) 
and gets called every time. 

Infinite recursive call keeps on increasing stack size, 
but stack size is limited. Hence at some point of time 
stack overflow occurs ie…. abnormal termination 
takes place. 

Understanding bar function: 

int bar(int val){ 

int x=0; 

while(val>0){ 

x=x+bar(val-1);} 

return val; 

} 

• 
We are still inside loop and val > 0 is true, bar (1) 
will again call bar(0) and again bar(0) will return 
0,  
• 
This process will continue ..  loop count wont be 
getting terminated. 
• 
But, stack size is not being exceeded at any 
moment of time. 
• 
Leading to infinite loop Hence, correct option is 
(c).

On tracing the tree from top to bottom and left to right 
we get 

3,1,2,2,1,3,4,4,4 

 Option a is correct answer.
Answer is option (d)

&&!   = 

Priority of && is less than !=  

if ((*a)) && (*a! = ' ')) 

if will become false, Either 

(1)  *a = 0 (ASCI to or \0) 

(2)  *a become ' ' 

Now, 

Recursion 'A'→'B'→'C'→'D'→ Recursion ends 
and then it  starts printing. 

Printing is done because putchar (*a); is written after 
recursive call. 

Hence, DCBA is printed, i.e. option D is correct.

x is a local variable so in every call x 
→


fun(1) =1 

fun(2) \Rightarrow = 1 

x = x + fun(1)*fun (2 -1) 


    = 1 + fun (1) * fun(1) = 1 + 1 \times 1 = 2 

fun(3) \Rightarrow k = 1, 2 

k = 1  

x = x + fun(1) * fun (2) + fun(2) *fun(1) 

   = 1 + 1 \times 2 + 2 \times 1 = 1 + 2 + 2 = 5 

fun (4) k = 1, 2, 3  n =4 

x = x + fun(1) \times fun(3) + fun(2) \times fun(2) + fun(3) \times 
fun(1) 

= 1 + 1 \times 5 + 2 ×2 + 5 \times 1 = 15 
k = 1, 2, 3, 4 n = 5 

fun(5) 

= 1 + fun(1) \times fun(4) + fun(2) \times fun(3) + fun(3) \times 
fun(2) + fun(4) \times fun(1) 

= 1 + 1 \times 15 + 2 \times 5 + 5 \times 2 + 15 \times 1 

= 1 + 15 + 10 + 10 + 15  

= 51

questions.subtopic: The return value of fun2 (5) is _________.
tags: The return value of fun2 (5) is _________.

solution.text:
xmain = xmain + f1( ) + f2() + f3() + f2(); 

f1() is called: 



26 is retuned by f1() 

xmain = xmain + f1( ) + f2() + f3() + f2(); 

     = 1 + 26 

f2() is called: 

There is local static variable which resides in memory 
throughout the program. 



51 is returned but x being static, still remains in 
memory. 

xmain = xmain + f1( ) + f2() + f3() + f2(); 


   = 1 + 26 + 51 

f3() is called: 

xglobal * = 10  

xglobal = 10 * 10 

xglobal = 100 

100 is returned 

xmain = xmain + f1( ) + f2() + f3() + f2(); 


   = 1 + 26 + 51 + 100 

f2 () is called again 

Variable x is already residing in memory already. 




xmain = xmain + f1( ) + f2() + f3() + f2(); 


   = 1 + 26 + 51 + 100 + 52= 230 

 230 is printed
